summary: Kind-blind satisfier set (roster/coverage/graph); recut requirement.kind into an each-grain 'satisfier is kind K' clause — wrong-kind satisfiers become findings, never silent exclusions

edit.description: Add one each-grain predicate to the closed `Predicate` vocabulary expressing 'this member is of the declared kind K' (enum variant + serde load — an unknown predicate is still rejected at load). This is the predicate the requirement's kind-narrowing clause evaluates over its (now kind-blind) satisfier set; a deliberate language change per contract.md 'clause' (the closed vocabulary is the authority).
Evaluate the new each-grain kind predicate at the `each` grain, and give it an admissibility arm (non-vacuous). Reuses the same predicate-eval path every other each-grain clause rides.

Recut `Requirement.kind` (compose.rs:74): it no longer narrows the candidate set (silent exclusion) — it sources the shipped each-grain 'every satisfier is kind K' clause over the requirement's opt-in selection. Update the field doc to the new role; a kind-blind requirement (no `kind`) attaches no narrowing clause.

`candidates_for`/`satisfiers_for` (roster.rs:44-74) draw kind-blind from every modeled kind — the opt-in `satisfies` join is the only filter. Recut admissibility (a) (roster.rs:174-183): a requirement whose narrowing names an unmodeled kind is a clause finding, never a silent 'can never be filled' exclusion. Check the each-grain kind clause here alongside count/unique/membership; `kind_label`/`any` narration follows.

The coverage satisfier stream draws kind-blind — a member of any modeled kind that opts in via `satisfies` counts toward the requirement, matching the unified roster/graph satisfier set (coverage.rs's by-kind flattening).

`degree` (graph.rs:260-276) drops the `let Some(kind) = &requirement.kind else { skip }` guard and ranges kind-blind over the satisfier set via the same `roster::is_satisfier` opt-in join — a degree bound no longer silently skips a kind-blind requirement.
Surface the new each-grain kind predicate in the generated JSON Schema so the prose-authoring keystroke path validates it.

Where the requirement kind's shipped default contract is constructed, wire the kind-narrowing clause the recut `Requirement.kind` sources (the mechanism ships with the requirement kind; the specific kind K stays per-requirement author data).

tests.asserts: A wrong-kind opt-in satisfier is a diagnostic (the each-grain kind clause fires), not silently excluded; a kind-blind requirement counts opt-ins of every modeled kind; an unmodeled narrowing kind surfaces as a clause finding via admissibility (a).
Coverage draws the satisfier set kind-blind — an opt-in of any modeled kind fills the requirement.
A `degree` bound on a kind-blind requirement ranges over its opt-in satisfiers rather than being skipped.
The new each-grain kind predicate parses/loads and round-trips in a requirement's clause set.
The kind-narrowing clause the recut requirement.kind sources appears as a declaration row in the lock.

acceptance: requirement.kind narrows via a shipped each-grain clause, not a second selector: a satisfier of the wrong kind is a finding, a kind-blind requirement is filled by any opt-in, and roster/coverage/graph.degree read one kind-blind satisfier set (contract.md 'selection', decision 0003).
notes: Resolves fork (requirement-satisfier-kind) per decision 0003. Adds one each-grain predicate to the closed vocabulary (a deliberate language change, contract.md 'clause') so 'satisfier is kind K' is expressible; requirement.kind sources that clause instead of narrowing candidates_for/degree. coverage/graph.degree/roster go kind-blind; roster admissibility (a) recut. One slice of d5d1b37; 0004 still un-derived.

summary: Enforcement mode moves from the SDK's hardcoded 'authority' assembly fact to a root-member field in the lock; temper guard reads its mode there; the hardcoded authority emit retires (0005).

edit.description: Retire the hardcoded `{ fact: "authority", value: "shared" }` the corpus never coined (declarations.ts:249, `assemblyFactRows`). Emit the root member's declared enforcement mode as a root-member declaration instead — harness-wide declarations are root-member fields (representation.md 'The root member'). Edge rows stay as they are.

`harness()` init and the `Harness` interface (assembly.ts:25-50) gain the enforcement-mode field on the root member, defaulting to advisory (temper fabricates no enforcement the author did not declare — intent.md law 4/invariant 5). The field flows into emit's root-member declaration.

The assertion `declarations.assembly == [{ fact: "authority", value: "shared" }]` (emit.test.ts:196) breaks — rewrite it to assert no uncoined `authority` fact is emitted and the root-member enforcement-mode field is declared and round-trips.

Recut `authority_from_lock` (main.rs:502): read the enforcement mode from the root-member field the lock now carries instead of `row.fact == "authority"`; a lock without the field (or a pre-field lock) defaults advisory. The `guard` dispatch (main.rs:341-344) sources its mode there. Comments/architecture cites in this region are stale — cut/retag on contact (rust.md exit clause).

The `Authority` type (compose.rs:18-32) is the retired 'surface-authority posture' concept name — rename it to the enforcement-mode concept per one-name-per-concept (intent.md invariant; spec-system.md 'One name per concept'), doc updated. Still populated from the lock's root-member field, not a coined assembly fact. Enum VALUES (shared/surface) are out of scope — see notes.

`guard()`/`GuardVerdict` (install.rs:574-597) consume the renamed enforcement-mode type — a mechanical rename; the warn/block dispatch behavior is unchanged this slice. Stale architecture cites in the guard-region comments ride this touch (rust.md exit clause).

tests.asserts: Emit produces no uncoined `authority` assembly fact; the root-member enforcement-mode field is declared and round-trips through the emit payload.
The root-member enforcement-mode field appears as a declaration row in the lock; the engine's mode read (guard) sources it from that field, and a lock lacking it defaults advisory.

acceptance: emit produces no 'authority' assembly fact; enforcement mode is a root-member field declared into the lock; temper guard sources its mode from that field (a lock without it defaults advisory) — representation.md 'The root member', decision 0005.
notes: Derives 0005 (a slice of d5d1b37): the home+concept move only. The 'authority'/'surface-authority' naming retires for 'enforcement mode' (one-name-per-concept). blockedBy SATISFIER-KIND-CLAUSE — both edit src/compose.rs. OUT OF SCOPE: the value vocabulary (shared/surface vs distribution.md's note/warn/block) is standing residue under distribution.md, not folded here; per-member mode override is deferred until guard blocks per mode (0005 Consequences). architecture/* cites ride.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest — nothing moved this window.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs still pins SDK `^0.0.2` (0.0.3 published) — a release-owned bump, not this sweep.
